title: Major Incident Notification Readiness to NCA (Payment Service)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'PSD2' → 'Art.96'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PSD2 Art.96 (Incident reporting) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.22.25: Major Incident Notification Readiness to NCA (Payment Service).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.22.25' running on index=payments pay:incident:milestone — incident_id, milestone, severity, impacted_psu_estimate, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=payments sourcetype="pay:incident:milestone" earliest=-90d
| eval inc_tag="nca"
| stats earliest(eval(if(milestone=="detected",_time,null()))) as t0,
        earliest(eval(if(milestone=="nca_notified",_time,null()))) as t1,
        dc(incident_id) as incidents by inc_tag, severity
| eval hours_to_nca=round((t1-t0)/3600,2)
| eval uc_id="22.22.25"
| where inc_tag="nca" AND (isnull(t1) OR hours_to_nca>24)
| sort - hours_to_nca
| table uc_id, severity, incidents, t0, t1, hours_to_nca
description: Supports incident governance, customer communications evidence, and supervisory reporting workflows associated with payment service continuity and fraud statistics.

grandmaExplanation: Supports incident governance, customer communications evidence, and supervisory reporting workflows associated with payment service continuity and fraud statistics — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=payments` `pay:incident:milestone` — incident_id, milestone, severity, impacted_psu_estimate.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=payments sourcetype="pay:incident:milestone" earliest=-90d
| eval inc_tag="nca"
| stats earliest(eval(if(milestone=="detected",_time,null()))) as t0,
        earliest(eval(if(milestone=="nca_notified",_time,null()))) as t1,
        dc(incident_id) as incidents by inc_tag, severity
| eval hours_to_nca=round((t1-t0)/3600,2)
| eval uc_id="22.22.25"
| where inc_tag="nca" AND (isnull(t1) OR hours_to_nca>24)
| sort - hours_to_nca
| table uc_id, severity, incidents, t0, t1, hours_to_nca
```

Understanding this SPL

**Major Incident Notification Readiness to NCA (Payment Service) (PSD2 / NCA operational expectations)** — Supports incident governance, customer communications evidence, and supervisory reporting workflows associated with payment service continuity and fraud statistics.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=payments` `pay:incident:milestone` — incident_id, milestone, severity, impacted_psu_estimate. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: payments; **sourcetype**: pay:incident:milestone. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=payments, sourcetype="pay:incident:milestone", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **inc_tag** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by inc_tag, severity** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hours_to_nca** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_id** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where inc_tag="nca" AND (isnull(t1) OR hours_to_nca>24)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **Major Incident Notification Readiness to NCA (Payment Service) (PSD2 / NCA operational expectations)**): table uc_id, severity, incidents, t0, t1, hours_to_nca


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (milestones), Table (late NCA notifications), Single value (mismatches)
